skill.name: auth
skill.description: Guides implementation of server-side authentication, route protection, role checks, and family-based data isolation for Next.js 15 and Supabase applications.
skill.summary: Implement Next.js and Supabase authentication with server-side checks, role controls, and tenant isolation.
skill.author: BOM-98

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, file trees, placeholders, and secure-auth recommendations. However, the skill documents collecting SSN, date of birth, address, and phone data during registration and storing SSN in auth user metadata. That creates a high privacy and compliance risk, so publication should wait until this guidance is removed or replaced.

high_findings.title: Sensitive Identity Data Stored in Auth Metadata
high_findings.description: The registration flow describes collecting SSN, date of birth, phone, and address data, then lists SSN under auth user metadata. Storing high-risk identity data in authentication metadata increases privacy, compliance, and exposure risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly mention SSN, date of birth, address, phone, and auth user metadata. This is a semantic data-protection issue, not only a static pattern match.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged backticks are Markdown fences, inline identifiers, and TypeScript examples that are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contents are Markdown documentation and diagrams. I found no executable shell script, Ruby backtick execution, or command dispatch path in these locations.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Environment and URL References
low_findings.description: The network and environment findings are documentation examples. The Supabase URL and localhost values are placeholders, and the file warns users not to commit environment files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines list required public configuration names and placeholder URLs. They do not read secrets, transmit credentials, or embed a real external endpoint.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Browser Storage Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and browser storage alerts appear to match authentication terminology and localStorage text. The referenced guidance recommends getUser token validation and httpOnly cookies instead of localStorage tokens.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content recommends server-side token revalidation and avoids browser token storage. I found no MD5, SHA1, custom crypto, or localStorage token storage implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Identity Data Stored in Auth Metadata
dangerous_patterns.description: The registration flow describes collecting SSN, date of birth, phone, and address data, then lists SSN under auth user metadata. Storing high-risk identity data in authentication metadata increases privacy, compliance, and exposure risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly mention SSN, date of birth, address, phone, and auth user metadata. This is a semantic data-protection issue, not only a static pattern match.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent server-side authentication patterns that prevent route leaks and tenant data exposure. This skill provides workflows for Supabase auth, protected routes, admin checks, and family-level isolation.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to protect Next.js App Router pages and layouts with server-side auth helpers.
Shows Server Action patterns for authenticated and admin-only operations.
Documents Supabase login, logout, registration, email verification, and GitHub OAuth flows.
Describes family-based tenant isolation using role and family access checks.
Summarizes middleware token refresh behavior and httpOnly cookie handling.
Provides reference files for auth file organization, flows, and security requirements.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install dependencies or modify application code by itself.
Examples assume an existing Next.js 15 App Router project with Supabase helpers already defined.
The current source includes unsafe sensitive-data guidance that should be corrected before use.
It does not replace a full security review of database policies, secrets, or production settings.

use_cases.title: Protect Application Routes
use_cases.description: Add server-side checks to pages, layouts, and Server Actions so unauthenticated users cannot access private screens.

use_cases.title: Implement Tenant Isolation
use_cases.description: Apply family-level access checks before reading or changing household data in a multi-tenant app.

use_cases.title: Review Auth Architecture
use_cases.description: Compare an existing Supabase auth setup against documented token validation, middleware, and role-control patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Protect a Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this auth skill to protect a new Next.js page from unauthenticated users. Explain the helper to use and where to call it.

prompt_templates.title: Secure a Server Action
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this auth skill to update a Server Action so it requires a signed-in user before changing profile data.

prompt_templates.title: Add Admin Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this auth skill to design admin-only access for a settings page and its related Server Actions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Tenant Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this auth skill to review a family-based data flow for cross-tenant access risks and missing authorization checks.

output_examples.input: I need to protect a dashboard page.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the server-side route helper, places the check at the start of the layout or page, and explains the redirect behavior for unauthenticated users.

output_examples.input: I need an admin-only settings action.
output_examples.output: The response recommends an admin authorization check before the mutation, describes expected failure handling, and notes that UI visibility is not authorization.

output_examples.input: I need to prevent family data leaks.
output_examples.output: The response maps the family identifier flow, requires server-side membership validation, and flags any direct trust in client-provided family IDs.

best_practices: Use server-side token validation for protected pages, layouts, Server Actions, and route handlers.
Enforce authorization on the server even when admin controls are hidden in the interface.
Remove SSN and other high-risk identity fields from auth metadata before using these patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on client-side role checks as the only authorization control.
Do not use session data on the server without revalidating the user token.
Do not store SSN, date of birth, or unnecessary identity data in auth user metadata.

faq.question: What framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js 15 App Router applications that use Supabase for authentication and session management.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with workflows, examples, and reference guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with role-based access control?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers admin and member checks for pages, Server Actions, and conditional interface rendering.

faq.question: Can it help with multi-tenant data isolation?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains family-based access checks that prevent users from accessing another family account.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk high?
faq.answer: The source describes collecting SSN and other sensitive identity data and storing SSN in auth metadata.

faq.question: What should be fixed before publishing?
faq.answer: Remove SSN and unnecessary sensitive personal data guidance, then document safer data minimization and storage practices.
